title: Using magnification formula for lenses
description: Let's practice problems involving the magnification formula for lenses.

assessment_items.question_content:
Newt is inspecting an injury on his $10\ \text{cm}$ tall pet beast Pickett by placing him $15\ \text{cm}$ to the left of a convex lens. Looking through the lens from the right, Newt sees a virtual image of Pickett form $60\ \text{cm}$ to the left of the lens.

**What is the image height, $h_{\text I}$, of Pickett?**   
*Note: Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$h_{\text I}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
**Strategy**   

For simple lenses, virtual images form on the same side as the object. So, Pickett's object and image distances will have the same signs. In the Cartesian sign convention, we know the following distances,

- object distance, $\goldD u=-15\ \text{cm}$,
- image distance, $\blueD v=-60\ \text{cm}$,
- object height, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}=+10\ \text{cm}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out the image height $h_{\text I}$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv{\dfrac{h_{\text I}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $h_{\text I}$** 
     
Substituting the values of $\blueD v$, $\goldD u$ and $\greenD{h_{\text O}}$ in the magnification formula gives us $h_{\text I}$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv\dfrac{h_{\text I}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}\\\\
h_{\text I}&=\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text O}\blueD{v}}}{\goldD u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\greenD{+10\ \text{cm}})(\blueD{-60\ \text{cm}})}{(\goldD{-15\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({+10\ \text{cm}})(\stackrel{4}{\cancel{-60\ \text{cm}}})}{(\underset{1}{\cancel{-15\ \text{cm}}})}\\\\
h_{\text I}&=+40\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

Pickett's image height is $h_{\text I}=+40\ \text{cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Star-Lord Peter Quill holds up a pocket convex lens $45\ \text{cm}$ in front of his little friend Baby Groot. Peter keeps his free hand $30\ \text{cm}$ from the lens on the other side and sees a sharp, $16\ \text{cm}$ tall inverted image of Baby Groot on his hand.

**What is the object height, $h_{\text O}$, of Baby Groot?**   
*Note: Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$h_{\text O}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
**Strategy**   

Baby Groot's object and image distances will have opposite signs as they are on different sides of the convex lens. In the Cartesian sign convention, we know the following distances,

- object distance, $\goldD u=-45\ \text{cm}$,
- image distance, $\blueD v=+30\ \text{cm}$,
- image height, $\greenD{h_{\text I}}=-16\ \text{cm}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out Baby Groot's height $h_{\text O}$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv{\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}{{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $h_{\text O}$** 
     
Substituting the values of $\blueD v$, $\goldD u$ and $\greenD{h_{\text I}}$ in the magnification formula gives us $h_{\text O}$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}{{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}\\\\
\dfrac{h_{\text O}}{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}&=\dfrac{\goldD{u}}{\blueD{v}}\\\\
h_{\text O}&=\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}\goldD{u}}{\blueD{v}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\greenD{-16\ \text{cm}})(\goldD{-45\ \text{cm}})}{(\blueD{+30\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({\stackrel{-8}{\cancel{-16}{\ \text{cm}}}})(\stackrel{-3}{\cancel{-45\ \text{cm}}})}{(\underset{\underset{1}{\cancel 2}}{\cancel{+30\ \text{cm}}})}\\\\
h_{\text O}&=+24\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

Baby Groot's height is $h_{\text O}=+24\ \text{cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marie is experimenting with a convex lens she needs to build a microscope. 

She places a pin $9.0\ \text{cm}$ to the left of the lens. A sharp, $20\ \text{cm}$ long inverted image forms on a screen kept $45\ \text{cm}$ to the right of the lens.

**What is the object height, $h_{\text O}$, of the pin?**   
*Note: Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$h_{\text O}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
**Strategy**   

The pin's object and image distances will have opposite signs as they are on different sides of the convex lens. In the Cartesian sign convention, we know the following distances,

- object distance, $\goldD u=-9.0\ \text{cm}$,
- image distance, $\blueD v=+45\ \text{cm}$,
- image height, $\greenD{h_{\text I}}=-20\ \text{cm}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out the pin's height $h_{\text O}$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv{\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}{{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $h_{\text O}$** 
     
Substituting the values of $\blueD v$, $\goldD u$ and $\greenD{h_{\text I}}$ in the magnification formula gives us $h_{\text O}$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}{{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{\goldD u}\\\\
\dfrac{h_{\text O}}{\greenD{h_{\text I}}}&=\dfrac{\goldD{u}}{\blueD{v}}\\\\
h_{\text O}&=\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text I}}\goldD{u}}{\blueD{v}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\greenD{-20\ \text{cm}})(\goldD{-9.0\ \text{cm}})}{(\blueD{+45\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({\stackrel{4}{\cancel{-20}{\ \text{cm}}}})(\stackrel{1}{\cancel{-9.0\ \text{cm}}})}{(\underset{\underset{1}{\cancel{-5}}}{\cancel{+45\ \text{cm}}})}\\\\
h_{\text O}&=+4.0\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The pin's height is $h_{\text O}=+4.0\ \text{cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bruce Wayne returns home and rings the doorbell standing $0.9\ \text{m}$ in front of the door, waiting for Alfred to let him in. Alfred looks through the peephole and sees a $0.6\ \text{m}$ tall image of Master Wayne, whom he knows to be $1.8\ \text{m}$ in height.

**Find the image distance, $v$, of Bruce Wayne as seen through the peephole**.           
*Note: Peepholes use concave lenses to give a wide view. Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$v=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints:
**Strategy** 
 
Let's write the known distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

- object distance, $\goldD u=-0.9\ \text{m}$,
- object height, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}=+1.8\ \text{m}$,
- image height, $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}=+0.6\ \text{m}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out the image distance $v$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv {\dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{v}{\goldD u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $v$** 

Substituting the values of $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}$, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}$ and $\goldD{u}$ in the magnification formula gives us $v$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv \dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{v}{\goldD u}\\\\
v&=\dfrac{\goldD{u}\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\goldD{-0.9\ \text{m}})(\purpleD{+0.6\ \text{m}})}{(\greenD{+1.8\ \text{m}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({-0.9}{\ \text{m}})(\stackrel{1}{\cancel{+0.6\ \text{m}}})}{(\underset{3}{\cancel{+1.8\ \text{m}}})}\\\\
v&=-0.3\ \text{m}
\end{align}$

The image distance, $v=-0.3\ \text{m}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In his secret laboratory, Dexter is experimenting with his spectacles that use concave lenses. 

He places a $12\ \text{cm}$ long pencil in front of one of the lenses, and observes a $3.0\ \text{cm}$ long virtual image of the pencil at a distance of $2.0\ \text{cm}$ from the lens.

**Find the object distance, $u$, of the pencil**.           
*Note: Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
**Strategy** 
 
Concave lenses always form virtual images on the same side of the lens as the object. Let's write the known distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

- object height, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}=+12\ \text{cm}$,
- image height, $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}=+3.0\ \text{cm}$,
- image distance, $\blueD v=-2.0\ \text{cm}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out the object distance $u$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv{\dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $u$** 

Substituting the values of $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}$, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}$ and $\blueD{v}$ in the magnification formula gives us $u$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv \dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{u}\\\\
\dfrac{u}{\blueD v}&=\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}\\\\
u&=\dfrac{\blueD v \greenD{h_{\text O}}}{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\blueD{-2.0\ \text{cm}}) (\greenD{+12\ \text{cm}})}{(\purpleD{+3.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({-2.0\ \text{cm}}) (\stackrel{4}{\cancel{+12\ \text{cm}}})}{(\underset{1}{\cancel{+3.0\ \text{cm}}})}\\\\
u&=-8.0\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The object distance of the pencil is $u=-8.0\ \text{cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lara Croft is testing concave lenses to make good eyepieces for her binoculars before the next mission.

In one experiment, Lara places a $14\ \text{cm}$ long pin to the left of a concave lens. She obtains a $2.0\ \text{cm}$ long virtual image at a distance of $3.0\ \text{cm}$ to the left of the lens.

**Find the object distance, $u$, of the pin**.           
*Note: Let's write the answer in the Cartesian sign convention.*

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
**Strategy** 
 
Let's write the known distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

- object height, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}=+14\ \text{cm}$,
- image height, $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}=+2.0\ \text{cm}$,
- image distance, $\blueD v=-3.0\ \text{cm}$.
 

Let's use the **magnification formula for lenses** to find out the object distance $u$,

$\begin{align}
m\equiv{\dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{u}}
\end{align}$

**Finding $u$** 

Substituting the values of $\purpleD{h_{\text I}}$, $\greenD{h_{\text O}}$ and $\blueD{v}$ in the magnification formula gives us $u$,

$\begin{align}
m&\equiv \dfrac{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}=\dfrac{\blueD v}{u}\\\\
\dfrac{u}{\blueD v}&=\dfrac{\greenD{h_{\text O}}}{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}\\\\
u&=\dfrac{\blueD v \greenD{h_{\text O}}}{\purpleD{h_{\text I}}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{(\blueD{-3.0\ \text{cm}}) (\greenD{+14\ \text{cm}})}{(\purpleD{+2.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{({-3.0\ \text{cm}}) (\stackrel{7}{\cancel{+14\ \text{cm}}})}{(\underset{1}{\cancel{+2.0\ \text{cm}}})}\\\\
u&=-21\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The object distance of the pencil is $u=-21\ \text{cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the science laboratory of *Xavier’s School for Gifted Youngsters*, a young Hank McCoy is doing an optics experiment with a concave lens and a pin as the object. He records the following distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

||
- | :-:
Object height, $h_{\text{O}}$ | $-6.5\ \text{cm}$
Image height, $h_{\text{I}}$ | $-1.3\ \text{cm}$


**What is the magnification, $m$, produced by the lens?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Vera is experimenting with a convex lens that she needs to build a telescope. In one experiment, she records the following distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

||
- | :-:
Object distance, $u$ | $-1.2\ \text{m}$
Image distance, $v$ | $+7.2\ \text{m}$

**What is the magnification, $m$, produced by the lens?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

Zoya decides to build her own projector to show her new film to friends, and needs a suitable convex lens.

In one of her experiments with a convex lens, she records the following distances in the Cartesian sign convention,

||
- | :-:
Object distance, $u$ | $-0.3\ \text{m}$
Image distance, $v$ | $+3.6\ \text{m}$

**What is the magnification, $m$, produced by the lens?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
